Optimus 3: Musk teases plans for unveilingTesla (TSLA) reported first quarter results on Wednesday after the closing bell. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $0.41 (compared to analyst estimates of $0.34), and revenue came in at $22.39 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $22.19 billion). On the earnings call, CEO Elon Musk projects that Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot will become the "biggest product ever," with production starting in Fremont later this year and expanding to Giga Texas by summer 2027.

Warsh commits to monetary policy independence, says president expressing views on rates isn't a threatFederal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh will tell Senate lawmakers Tuesday that setting interest rates independently is "essential," but that the president's views on monetary policy don't threaten central bank independence.
